title: Water ATM — Coin-Triggered Flow Pump Dispenser
design_question: How to dispense a metered volume of purified water upon coin insertion and fill a bottle automatically?
selected_approach: Coin acceptor (CH-926, accepts ₹1/₹2/₹5) triggers Arduino Uno. Solenoid valve (NC, 12 V) opens 12 V peristaltic pump for calibrated time (e.g. 1 litre = 60 s at 1 L/min). YF-S201 flow sensor (1–30 L/min, 450 pulses/L) measures actual volume. LCD 16×2 displays selected volume and status. Bottle sensor (IR photogate) confirms bottle present before dispensing. Overflow: timer fallback cuts off after 120 s.
rationale: Peristaltic pump is food-safe, self-priming, easy to clean (only silicone tube contacts water). Coin acceptor handles multiple denominations. Flow sensor provides volumetric accuracy ±2%. Arduino logic is straightforward.

alternatives.reason_rejected: Higher cost for same flow rate.

verification_notes: FACT: 'Vending Machine Mechanisms — Coin Validation and Dispensing' covers coin acceptor validation protocol.
supporting_fact_titles: Coin Acceptor — Pulse-Count Authentication and Relay Dispense
